# tg_cluster_service (Resource)

Manage an L4 service on a cluster (V2). Requires the cluster to have been upgraded to V2 services config — see `tg_cluster_services_v2_upgrade`.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "tg_cluster_service" "https_forwarder" {
  cluster_fqdn = "hq.example.test"
  name         = "https-forwarder"
  protocol     = "tcp"
  host         = "10.20.30.40"
  port         = 443
  enabled      = true

  source_interface       = "ens192"
  source_from_cluster_ip = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `cluster_fqdn` (String) FQDN of the cluster that owns this service.
- `host` (String) Destination host.
- `name` (String) Service name.
- `port` (Number) Destination port (1-65535).
- `protocol` (String) Protocol. One of: udp, tcp, tftp, ftp, rdp, vnc, ssh.

### Optional

- `description` (String) Description.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Whether the service is enabled.
- `source_from_cluster_ip` (Boolean) When true, bind the outbound socket to the cluster VIP on `source_interface`. Requires `source_interface` to be set. V2-only.
- `source_interface` (String) NIC used for the upstream connection (e.g. `ens192`). V2-only.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The ID of this resource.
- `service_id` (String) Service unique ID. Computed after create.
